commit.message:
fix: resolve all TypeScript build errors for Vercel deployment

- Fix tsconfig extends to use relative paths (TS doesn't honour exports field)
- Add typesVersions to @pulse/db and @pulse/config packages
- Add Relationships: [] and CompositeTypes to database.types.ts (Supabase JS v2.103 requirement)
- Fix Insert types for organizations, organization_members, responses, response_answers
  to make nullable/defaulted columns optional (matches real Supabase generator output)
- Export Json type from @pulse/db index
- Fix implicit any on setAll cookie params in server.ts and middleware.ts
- Add missing component stubs: auth forms, dashboard shell, survey runner, kiosk runner, etc.
- Add next-intl i18n config (src/i18n/request.ts, routing.ts, messages/en.json)
- Add increment_distribution_count RPC to database types

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Claude/saas platform setup l zf6 h (#112)

* fix(core.js): rename install_confirmed → install for dashboard compatibility

The core.js PWA tracking was sending "install_confirmed" but most dashboards
(including le-resto/espace-admin.html) expect "install". Changed the event
name to "install" so install counts appear correctly in all dashboards.

Also updated 1stat.html to count both "install" and "install_confirmed"
(legacy) to handle existing data in the spreadsheet.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016wF27YkF21e7Pdv4o9MFtn

* fix(tracking): 2 bugs critiques signup/install à 0

1. core.js — track() envoyait le NOM du restaurant (ex: "Le coreen")
   au lieu du SLUG (ex: "le-coreen") comme clé `resto`.
   Résultat : scans matchaient (avant loadConfig) mais signup/install
   ne matchaient pas (après loadConfig) → affichés à 0 dans dashboard.
   Fix : track() utilise toujours getRestoSlug().

2. espace-admin.html (tous restaurants) — dashboard cherchait
   s.install_confirmed mais core.js envoie s.install depuis le fix précédent.
   Fix : s.install ?? s.install_confirmed (accept les deux formats).

Fichiers modifiés : assets/core.js + 8× */admin/espace-admin.html

https://claude.ai/code/session_016wF27YkF21e7Pdv4o9MFtn

* fix: show PIN validation code directly on dashboard tab

Staff can now see the dynamic PIN code immediately when they open the
admin without having to navigate to the Coupon tab.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016wF27YkF21e7Pdv4o9MFtn

* fix: make coupon KPI card clickable to navigate to coupon tab

Clicking "Coupon du mois" on the dashboard now navigates to the Coupon tab.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016wF27YkF21e7Pdv4o9MFtn

* fix: show coupon validation count as number in dashboard KPI card

The coupon KPI card now shows the count of coupon validations this month
as a number (like other KPI cards), with "Actif/Inactif" as a small status.

https://claude.ai/code/session_016wF27YkF21e7Pdv4o9MFtn

* feat: système complet abonnement Stripe + facturation admin

Ajout du système SaaS complet pour Fidelavis :

Assets
- assets/plans.js : config Stripe, définition offres (Essentiel 97€/Pro 149€),
  helpers isPro(), hasFeature(), getPlanLabel(), formatPrice(), getStatusLabel()
- assets/billing.js : FidelavisBilling.startCheckout() via GAS proxy,
  openPortal() pour portail Stripe, initCheckoutButtons() auto-bind [data-plan]
- assets/admin-billing.js : FidelavisAdminBilling.renderBilling() — lit config.json
  et affiche compte, abonnement, statut, factures dans l'admin

Google Apps Script
- apps-script/stripe/stripeCheckout.gs : proxy GAS → crée Checkout Sessions Stripe
  (setup 199€ + abonnement) et sessions portail client
- apps-script/stripe/stripeWebhook.gs : webhook Stripe — gère
  checkout.session.completed, invoice.paid, invoice.payment_failed,
  subscription.deleted, subscription.updated → Google Sheet + emails
- apps-script/pennylane/pennylaneSync.gs : facturation électronique Pennylane,
  createSetupInvoice(), createSubscriptionInvoice(), syncPendingInvoices()

Admin client
- _template/admin/billing.html + le-coreen/admin/billing.html :
  page "Compte & Facturation" avec sections Compte, Abonnement, Factures,
  bouton "Gérer mon abonnement" (portail Stripe)
- espace-admin.html (template + le-coreen) : ajout onglet "💳 Compte" (admin only)
- _template/config.json : ajout champs billing (plan, subscriptionStatus,
  setupPaid, stripeCustomerId, stripeSubscriptionId, nextBillingDate, invoices)

Documentation
- apps-script/stripe/BILLING-SETUP.md : guide complet de configuration

https://claude.ai/code/session_016wF27YkF21e7Pdv4o9MFtn

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix OpenAI Realtime: add LLMContextAggregatorPair + LLMRunFrame (from official example)

OpenAI Realtime was missing context aggregators and LLMRunFrame.
The official example (19-openai-realtime.py) shows it needs:
- LLMContext with greeting
- LLMContextAggregatorPair with SileroVADAnalyzer
- Pipeline: input → user_aggregator → service → output → assistant_aggregator
- LLMRunFrame on connect
- AudioConfiguration with SemanticTurnDetection

Without these, the service connected but never processed audio.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add .gitignore with Terraform entries to exclude large provider binaries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve Gemini model compatibility and enhance realtime chat

- Fix "Thinking level not supported" error by conditionally applying thinkingConfig only to Gemini 3.x models (gemini-3-flash-preview, gemini-3.1-pro-preview, gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview)
- Add THINKING_MODELS set to identify models that support thinkingLevel parameter
- Enhance realtime chat with global conversations subscription for sidebar auto-refresh
- Add message deduplication in realtime subscription to prevent duplicate messages
- Improve auto-scroll reliability with requestAnimationFrame
- Add userRef for stable reference in realtime callbacks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add mobile OTP verification and Google OAuth to registration/login

Backend:
- OTPRecord + GoogleAuthRequest + GoogleCompleteRequest models
- phone_verified field on User; password_hash optional for Google accounts
- POST /auth/send-otp — generates 6-digit OTP, sends via Twilio (logs
  to console in dev if Twilio not configured), rate-limited to 3/5min
- POST /auth/register — now requires otp query param, verifies before
  creating account with phone_verified=true
- POST /auth/google — verifies Google access token via userinfo endpoint,
  returns JWT for existing users or needs_phone=true for new users
- POST /auth/google/complete — verifies OTP then creates Google-linked account
- TTL index on otp_records.expires_at (auto-deletes after 5 min)
- Added twilio, google-auth, httpx to requirements.txt

Frontend:
- AuthModal rewritten with 3-step registration flow:
  Step 1: name/email/phone/password → Send OTP
  Step 2: 6-box OTP input with paste support + resend countdown
  Step 3: account created, logged in
- Google OAuth button on both login and register tabs
- New Google users shown phone collection + OTP step before account creation
- @react-oauth/google installed; App.js wrapped with GoogleOAuthProvider

Env vars needed on Render:
  TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER
  GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID (same value needed in Vercel as REACT_APP_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: disclose NEXT_LOCALE functional cookie in cookie policy

The language switcher sets a NEXT_LOCALE cookie (1-year expiry) that
was not disclosed. Add it as a functional cookie category and correct
the false claim that no functional cookies are used.

- Made Generate Strategy button emerald-colored by default
- Added Run Backtest functionality with loading state

commit.message:
i18n(de): rework German copy for natural phrasing and tone

Replace literal English-to-German calques with idiomatic German
throughout the marketing copy — fixes staccato fragments that don't
work in German, removes the anglicism "Null" as informal zero,
replaces "KI-Belegschaft" (non-existent compound) with "KI-Agenten",
fixes "rein/raus" slang inconsistent with formal Sie-form, corrects
typo "Anfragervolumen" → "Anfragevolumen", replaces ambiguous
"keine Optionen" with "nicht verhandelbar", and replaces the
"Kontaktformular-Einsendungen" calque with "Kontaktanfragen"
throughout Privacy copy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add AI persona system, auth, chat redesign, and media sharing

- Add 6 AI characters (Sara, Aman, Surbhi, Harry, Spider-Man, Alex) with
  unique personalities, backstories, and different Gemini models per character
  (gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-3-flash-preview, gemini-3.1-pro-preview)
- Add mood-aware system prompts for natural human-like responses
- Redesign landing page to showcase app purpose and new characters
- Add email/password auth with Supabase (login, signup, email verification)
- Add profile setup page (name, age, bio, avatar upload)
- Redesign chat page WhatsApp-style with sidebar contacts and media sharing

- Add Supabase migration SQL with profiles, conversations, messages tables
- Fix storage policy naming conflicts for Supabase
- Add .env.example for deployment reference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(harness-tests): v5 direct driver + me.consider, viewer doc, continuity header

v5: keeps v4 architecture but adds me.consider(prompt) — cheap inference,
text in/text out, no edit tools. Default on_tick uses consider every tick.
Counts against same 50-turn budget as reflectOn.

Smoke tests:
- v5-sonnet: composite 7 (game 25, died to grue at step 18)
- v5-opus: composite -10 (game 40, full 50-turn budget burn at step 50)

Direct driver hits cellar much faster (~step 11) but burns budget per
tick. v4-opus's composite 17 still beats v5-opus's -10 — navigator with
deliberate reflection wins on cost-efficiency.

Refined v5 defaults:
- goal: "get a high score in this game"
- memory: "just woke up here, not sure how things work (yet)"
- (was a verbose API explanation)

Also:
- New doc: docs/playthrough-viewer.md — how to open the viewer, window API
- Journal continuity header at the top — "where we are right now" for
  fresh context windows. Captures architecture, run command, all v0-v5
  generations, key findings, open threads.
- Journal session 16 (v5 results)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Document MEM700 RAM disk: schematic, I/O ports, DMA, boot protocol

Comprehensive documentation of the MEM700 static RAM disk board from
the RC702tech.pdf schematic (physical p.219) and PHE358A.MAC source.

Covers: hardware (two 61256 SRAMs, 64KB, 74HC02 glue logic), I/O ports
(0xEE status/track, 0xEF sector+DMA trigger), DMA channel 0 protocol,
disk geometry (1024B sectors, 16 sectors/track), format routine, and the
full CP/M boot sequence from MEM700.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Document SEM702 font compression options for PROM embedding

Analysis of approaches to compress the 2048-byte font to fit alongside
boot code in a 4KB PROM. Best practical option: skip unused scan lines
+ skip blank characters = ~1072 bytes with trivial decompressor.

commit.message:
Document uncertainty about PROM1 font ROM identity and bit order

We assumed PROM1 contains an ROA327 image, but the actual content is
unknown. The bit-reversal in PHE358A suggests MSB-first source data,
but if the PROM1 font is already in SEM702-native order, the reversal
would produce mirrored characters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: rc700-gensmedet
repository.description: Reforging complete sources for the Regnecentralen RC702 Piccolo system.

commit.message:
Add RC702tech.pdf with OCR, update SEM702 documentation

Added RC702tech.pdf (product documentation including SEM702 functional
description and Comal80 programming example). OCR text layer added.

Updated load_chargen() comment with product context: SEM702 is
"Semigrafik Memory", a RAM replacement for the ROA327 ROM character
generator in IC82. Bit-reversal is needed when loading from the
ROA327 ROM (MSB-first) because SEM702 expects LSB-first. Direct
programming (as in the Comal80 example) needs no reversal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add English/German internationalization via next-intl

Introduces full i18n support targeting German industrial manufacturers.

- Install next-intl v4; configure plugin in next.config.js
- Add messages/en.json and messages/de.json covering all marketing copy
  (Hero, Problem, HowItWorks, Benefits, Regulations, Security, CTA,
  Header, Footer, CookieConsent)
- Middleware (src/middleware.ts) detects Accept-Language and redirects
  German browsers to /de/; sets NEXT_LOCALE cookie used by root layout
- Root layout reads NEXT_LOCALE cookie and provides NextIntlClientProvider
  so every component (including Header, Footer, CookieConsent) is locale-aware
- Move main page to src/app/[locale]/page.tsx; locale layout adds
  hreflang alternate tags and German-specific SEO metadata

  src/i18n/navigation.ts createNavigation wrapper
- Translate GameHero in regulation-sweeper/page.tsx (reuses Hero namespace)
- Remove unused Pricing section components

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix SDCC build: declare z80_delay_ms locally instead of #include <z80.h>

z88dk's <z80.h> header uses __smallc/__z88dk_fastcall qualifiers that
zsdcc's --c1mode --sdcccall 1 pipeline doesn't handle correctly. The
header gets preprocessed by ucpp but the qualifiers are lost when
zsdcc re-parses the output, resulting in z80_delay_ms being treated
as an implicit void->int function. Passing a parameter then triggers
"error 101: too many parameters".

Fix: declare z80_delay_ms with a plain extern prototype instead.

Also fixes the boot_rom.c:89 unreachable code warning context (BSS_SIZE
is 0 at compile time, so the memset is optimized away — harmless).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add SEM 702 character generator loader to autoload PROM

Ported LDGEN routine from PHE358A.MAC (RC702E PROM source) to the C
autoload PROM. Loads 128 characters × 16 dot lines from PROM1 (0x2000)
into the SEM 702 character generator RAM via ports 0xD1–0xD3, with
bit-reversal (font ROM is MSB-first, SEM 702 expects LSB-first).

A copy of the ROA296 character generator ROM must be installed in the
PROM1 socket for this to work.

Called after init_crt() and before display use, matching the PHE358A
boot sequence. PROM1 must still be mapped when this runs (before
prom_disable()).

Clang PROM: 1756 → 1829 bytes (+73B). Still within 2KB limit.

Also adds the clean-room BIOS specification document.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add comprehensive API response normalizer for Python backend compatibility

The Python backend returns snake_case field names while the frontend expects
camelCase. Rather than fixing each endpoint individually, this adds a central
normalizeApiResponse() function applied to ALL JSON responses from request(),
login(), and register().

Normalizations applied:
- User: name→firstName/lastName, is_admin→isAdmin, tier→subscriptionTier,
  created_at→createdAt, updated_at→updatedAt
- Project: user_id→userId, journey_type→journeyType, journey_progress→
  journeyProgress, analysis_results→analysisResults, is_paid→isPaid,
  payment_status→paymentStatus, record_count→recordCount
- Dataset: source_type→sourceType, original_file_name→originalFileName,
  ingestion_metadata→ingestionMetadata, sample_data→sampleData
- Pagination: total_count→totalCount, total_pages→totalPages

Also sends both camelCase and snake_case in register request body so
both Python and Node.js backends can parse user registration.

Removes duplicate login-specific normalization (now handled generically).

fix: resolve login failure, WebSocket 403 spam, and health check 404

Three issues from manual testing with Python backend:

1. Auth response mapping (api.ts):
   Python backend returns {name, is_admin, tier} but frontend expects
   {firstName, lastName, isAdmin, subscriptionTier}. Added normalization
   layer in login() that maps snake_case/Python fields to camelCase.
   Fixes: "Authentication succeeded but no user data received"

2. WebSocket reconnection spam (realtime.ts):
   WS was reconnecting endlessly on 403 (no auth token before login).
   Now skips reconnection when no token is available and auto-connects
   via 'auth-token-stored' event after successful login.
   Fixes: Repeated "connection rejected (403 Forbidden)" in backend logs

3. Health check endpoint (ServiceHealthBanner.tsx):
   Was calling /api/system/health (→ /system/health on Python backend)
   which returns 404. Changed to /api/health (→ /health) matching
   Python backend's actual endpoint. Added catch fallback.
   Fixes: "/system/health 404 Not Found" in backend logs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix phantom quota failures in clause/background extraction

Two problems caused ~1,089 deals to be incorrectly marked
clause_extraction_status='failed' during the 2026-04-10 overnight run:

1. extract_batch_via_cli in clause_extractor.py caught the quota-exhausted
   RuntimeError with a bare `except Exception` and returned empty results,
   so the outer loop marked every deal in the batch as failed instead of
   stopping. Re-raise RuntimeError before the catch-all, matching the
   already-correct pattern in background_extractor.py.

2. Neither extractor persisted the "paused until" state, so the shell
   auto-relaunch loop kept triggering new runs during the 10-hour quota
   window and each one burned through another 200 phantom failures.

Adds app/utils/quota_pause.py: a shared file-based sentinel (/tmp by default)
that parses the "resets Xpm (UTC)" reset time from the CLI error payload and
persists it. Both extractors now:
  - Short-circuit run_*_extraction if a pause is active
  - Check the pause file between batches so a sibling pipeline can halt them
  - Record the pause when they detect a quota error, before raising

clause_extraction_loop.sh now reads quota_paused / quota_paused_until from
the run response and sleeps until the reset time (+60s buffer) instead of
polling every 60s.

tools/rollback_phantom_quota_failures.py rolls failed statuses back to NULL
for the affected time window. Dry-run by default, --execute writes. Defaults
to the 2026-04-10 01:15–02:00 UTC window (1,089 clause failures, 0 background).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
